rationale_one_paragraph: The Vercel AI SDK was being used as a state machine for a deterministic 10-step wizard, but lite/mini models couldn't reliably emit follow-up tool calls so 19 of 19 wizard cards were already being rendered client-side via a CLIENT_RENDERED_DIRECTIVES allowlist. Two of three Edge Functions were already bypassing the LLM. Net: the LLM did nothing in the main flow but the codebase paid full chat-stream lifecycle cost. This refactor moves to a row-as-truth server-state-driven wizard: each step is a Server Action that writes the row and revalidates the page; the LLM is invoked only for Step 7 diagnostic Q&A and Step 10.3 customer-notes parsing. Chat bubbles stay as decoration (deterministic templates).

context_protocol.rule_1_one_phase_per_turn: Work ONE phase per turn. Do not chain phases in the same turn even if they look small.
context_protocol.rule_2_end_of_phase: When a phase is done: (1) set its status to 'complete'; (2) set completed_at to today's ISO date; (3) advance current_phase to the next pending phase; (4) write a one-line completion note in the phase's 'completion_note' field; (5) STOP and prompt the user before continuing.
context_protocol.rule_3_read_narrowly: Read only the slice of chat-design.md relevant to the current phase (use the section_refs the phase declares). Don't reload the whole 3,278-line spec each turn.
context_protocol.rule_4_ask_in_grey_areas: When in doubt about scope, naming, or approach, ASK the user before guessing. Per .claude/rules/never-guess.md.
context_protocol.rule_5_no_regressions: /book must keep working through phases 3-14. New wizard surface lives at /book-v2 until phase 15 swaps the routes.
context_protocol.rule_6_verify_locally: Each phase must end with `npm run typecheck` passing in scheduler-app/. Tests don't need to all pass yet during phases 3-14 (the old AI-SDK-shaped tests will be migrated in phase 17).
context_protocol.rule_7_commit_per_phase: Each phase produces one git commit. User opens PRs; do not push unless asked.

phases.title: Independent bug fixes (4)
phases.completion_note: All 4 fixes landed. Bug 1: verifyOtp now accepts optional session_id; on verified=true writes otp_verified_at + identity_verification_level='full' + last_active_at to customer_chat_sessions. scheduler-tools.ts verify_otp tool threads sessionId from getSchedulerTools closure. Bug 2: dismissEscalation now orders by occurred_at (the actual column). Bug 3: computeAvailableDates now applies the full 5-layer availability stack (closed_dates + appointment_default_limits.is_closed + appointment_blocks full + appointment_blocks time-specific + holds/appointments count vs capacity). Bug 4: listWaiterTimes now uses Intl.DateTimeFormat in America/New_York to bucket appointments by shop-local hour — correct in both EDT and EST. The hardcoded -04:00 day window was replaced with a 1-day buffer queried in UTC then filtered by shop-local date. npm run typecheck passes clean. Unit test for verifyOtp persistence DEFERRED to phase_17 (test suite migration) — adding a Vitest unit test for a Deno-side function requires Supabase-mock infrastructure that doesn't fit Phase 1's scope.
phases.summary: Fix the 4 verified bugs whose surface area outlives the migration. Each is small; bundle them in one phase. SKIP bugs that touch the chat-stream layer being removed — those get fixed inside their respective migration phase.
tasks: Bug 1: verifyOtp at supabase/functions/_shared/tools/scheduler-otp.ts:477-483 — on verified=true, write otp_verified_at=now() + identity_verification_level='full' on the customer_chat_sessions row. Accept session_id as an extra param or look up by phone_e164 within the function (decide at execution).

Bug 2: dismissEscalation at scheduler-app/src/lib/scheduler/actions/session-actions.ts:3020 — change .order('created_at') to .order('occurred_at'). The scheduler_audit_log column is occurred_at per migration 20260513000100.

Bug 3: computeAvailableDates at scheduler-app/src/lib/scheduler/actions/session-actions.ts:1972-2110 — apply capacity exclusion. For each candidate date: count active appointment_holds + non-cancelled appointments per appointment_type; exclude the date when total >= sum-of-capacity-for-that-day's-time-slots (waiter: waiter_8am_slots + waiter_9am_slots; dropoff: dropoff_total). Reuse the math pattern from hold_waiter_slot RPC if useful.

Bug 4: listWaiterTimes at supabase/functions/scheduler-booking-direct/index.ts:363-385 — replace hardcoded UTC hour=12/13 (assumes EDT) with shop-timezone-aware comparison. Phase 1 single-shop: use America/New_York via Intl.DateTimeFormat or date-fns-tz. Lift the timezone string to a const so phase 2 multi-shop can read from a column later.

phases.title: New wizard primitives (no UI swap)
phases.completion_note: Five new files under scheduler-app/src/lib/scheduler/wizard/. (1) card-payloads.ts — WizardCard discriminated union covering all 25 step values with per-step payload interfaces; includes a compile-time exhaustiveness check (_StepCoverageCheck). (2) transition-types.ts — minimal WizardTransitionResult ({ok:true, next_step} | {ok:false, error}). (3) append-bubble.ts — writes a single { type:'text', text } part row to customer_chat_messages; resolves shop_id from the session row; best-effort with Sentry+console on failure (does not throw). (4) transition.ts — applyWizardTransition writes the row, optionally appends user+assistant bubbles, and revalidatePath('/book-v2'). Phase 15 changes the revalidate target to '/book'. (5) get-current-card.ts — full 25-case switch from row state to WizardCard. Phase 2 fills in the trivial cases (greeting, phone_name, otp_pending, etc.) and stubs Tekmetric-touching cases with defensive placeholder payloads + TODO(phase_NN) markers naming the phase that will fill them in. Includes JSONB parsers for edited_phones/edited_emails/edited_address. npm run typecheck passes clean. Unit tests DEFERRED to phase_17 (test infra has pre-existing module-resolution issues separate from this phase).
phases.summary: Add types + helpers for the server-state-driven wizard. Old AI-SDK surface stays untouched. Goal: enable the upcoming step-migration phases without touching /book.
tasks: Create scheduler-app/src/lib/scheduler/wizard/card-payloads.ts — TypeScript discriminated union WizardCard = { step: 'greeting'; payload: GreetingPayload } | { step: 'phone_name'; payload: PhoneNamePayload } | ... for all 24 current_step values. Per-step payload types describe what the card needs to render.

Create scheduler-app/src/lib/scheduler/wizard/get-current-card.ts — async function getCurrentCard(chatId): Promise<WizardCard | null>. Reads the row, builds payload from row state (e.g., for 'customer_info_edit' it loads phones/emails/address from the row OR triggers a Tekmetric lookup if not yet stashed).

Create scheduler-app/src/lib/scheduler/wizard/transition-types.ts — type WizardTransitionResult = { ok: true; next_step: WizardStep; jeff_says?: string } | { ok: false; error: string; jeff_says?: string }.

Create scheduler-app/src/lib/scheduler/wizard/append-bubble.ts — async function appendBubble(chatId, role, text). Writes a simplified row to customer_chat_messages: { session_id, shop_id, role, parts: [{ type: 'text', text }], created_at }. Coexists with the existing AI-SDK-shaped rows during the migration; phase 16 prunes the AI-SDK shape.

Create scheduler-app/src/lib/scheduler/wizard/transition.ts — helper applyWizardTransition({ chatId, updates, next_step, jeff_says? }): writes row + appends bubble + calls revalidatePath('/book-v2'). Used by every step's Server Action.
Unit tests for getCurrentCard against several fixture rows (greeting, phone_name, otp_pending, vehicle_pick, summary).

phases.title: Step 1 (Greeting) on new pattern
phases.completion_note: Three new files. (1) app/book-v2/page.tsx — Server Component: hydrateSession (cookie → chatId) → ensureSessionExists (idempotent row INSERT) → getCurrentCard (build WizardCard from row state) → renders WizardSurface inside a Heritage-Editorial layout matching /book's chrome. dynamic='force-dynamic' so cookie hydration runs on every request. (2) src/components/scheduler/wizard/WizardSurface.tsx — Client Component switching on card.step. Phase 3 wires only the 'greeting' case to GreetingCard (reusing the existing heritage/GreetingCard verbatim — only the onSubmit handler changes); every other step renders NotYetMigrated pointing back at /book. Errors from the V2 Server Action are logged to Sentry + console for phase 3; phase 14 replaces with inline error UX. (3) src/lib/scheduler/wizard/actions/submit-greeting.ts — 'use server' submitGreetingV2 Server Action: writes is_returning_customer + customer_self_identified + greeting_answered_at, advances current_step → 'phone_name' via applyWizardTransition, appends a Jeff-voice bubble varying by greeting bucket (returning/new/unsure), returns WizardTransitionResult. Bubble copy is inline (not delegated to the legacy bubble-templates.ts which is destined for deletion in phase 16). /book legacy route is untouched. npm run typecheck passes clean. phases.title: Step 2 (PhoneName) on new pattern
phases.completion_note: Step 2 (phone + first + last name) is now live on /book-v2. (1) NEW src/lib/scheduler/wizard/actions/submit-phone-name.ts — 'use server' submitPhoneNameV2: Zod-validates (first/last 1-50 chars, phone E.164 +1XXXXXXXXXX), pre-writes entered_first_name + entered_last_name + phone_e164 to the row so values survive a downstream failure, reads customer_self_identified, calls callStep2Direct (deterministic edge function — no LLM), maps the 6 possible directives to WizardSteps (send_otp_first→otp_pending, show_no_match_choose_path→no_match_choose_path, show_multi_account_disambiguation→multi_account_disambiguation, show_partial_verification_gate→partial_verification_gate, show_new_customer_info_card / show_new_customer_form→new_customer_info, show_escalation_card→escalated), hands off to applyWizardTransition with branch-specific updates + bubble copy. Fail-safe escalation when step2-direct throws or returns an unknown directive. (2) UPDATE PhoneNamePayload — added optional initial_first_name / initial_last_name / initial_phone_e164 for resume / branch bounce-back prefill. (3) UPDATE getCurrentCard — phone_name case now populates prefill fields from row; partial_verification_gate case fixed to default matched_axis='name' (the only branch step2-direct emits, per chat-design.md §3.5b) with matched_first_name=null for PII protection per spec line 217. (4) UPDATE WizardSurface — added phone_name case wiring heritage/PhoneNameCard; refactored the per-action error log into a shared logIfFailed helper so future per-step cases stay DRY. Branch-state cards (multi-account, partial, no-match, escalated, new_customer_info) still render NotYetMigrated — phase 5 wires them. npm run typecheck passes clean. Manual smoke test for the user: visit /book-v2, tap a greeting bucket → PhoneNameCard renders with the right step_label per bucket → submit a real test phone → verify (a) row.phone_e164 / entered_*_name written before step2-direct call, (b) directive maps correctly to a next step, (c) OTP SMS arrives for the send_otp_first path.

phases.title: Step 3 OTP + §3.5a partial + §3.5b no-match (Option B: OTP for new customers)
phases.policy_decision: Option B chosen by Chris 2026-05-13: new customers receive OTP too. scheduler-step2-direct's 'new/unsure + 0-match' branch now emits send_otp_first instead of show_new_customer_info_card. submitOtpV2 branches on row.customer_id==NULL post-verify to route to new_customer_info (Step 4 new-client) instead of customer_info_edit.
phases.completion_note: Six new files + 2 edits. (1) NEW supabase/functions/scheduler-otp-direct/ — Deno edge function with op='verify' and op='resend' ops. Verify reads phone + customer_id off the session row, calls the existing verifyOtp helper (Phase 1 fix writes otp_verified_at + identity_verification_level='full' on success), bumps session-level otp_attempts on failure, returns { verified, customer_id, attempts_remaining }. Resend calls sendOtp (rate-limit + Telnyx), resets otp_attempts to 0. (2) NEW deno.json for the edge function. (3) NEW scheduler-app/src/lib/scheduler/otp-direct-client.ts — Next.js Pattern-A-bearer fetch wrapper with typed response unions for both ops. (4) NEW wizard/actions/submit-otp.ts — submitOtpV2: Zod-validates 6-digit code, calls callOtpVerify, branches on verified + customer_id (customer_id set → customer_info_edit; null → new_customer_info per Option B), bumps to escalated on attempts_remaining=0, stays on otp_pending with attempts-remaining bubble on wrong code. (5) NEW wizard/actions/resend-otp.ts — resendOtpV2: calls callOtpResend, escalates on rate_limited / send_failed. (6) NEW wizard/actions/submit-no-match-choice.ts — try_different_phone clears phone/OTP and bounces to phone_name with name preserved; continue_as_new sets bucket='new' + advances to new_customer_info WITHOUT additional OTP (per spec §3.5b — the choose-path screen is the verification proxy). (7) NEW wizard/actions/submit-partial-verification-choice.ts — 4 actions: use_different_phone (clear + bounce), proceed_as_partial (set level='partial' + skip directly to vehicle_pick per spec lines 751-758), continue_as_new (bucket flip), escalate. (8) EDIT supabase/functions/scheduler-step2-direct/index.ts — Option B implementation: new/unsure + 0-match now emits send_otp_first instead of show_new_customer_info_card. Updated comment block to reflect the policy decision. (9) EDIT WizardSurface.tsx — added OtpInput, PartialVerificationGateCard, NoMatchChoosePathCard cases. Multi-account case still renders NotYetMigrated (phase_05b). npm run typecheck passes clean. Verification ready: full Step 1 → 2 → 3 → customer_info_edit placeholder (returning); full Step 1 → 2 → 3 → new_customer_info placeholder (new + 0-match via Option B); 3 wrong codes → escalated; resend works after 30s cooldown; partial + no-match cards route per spec.

phases.title: Step §3.5c (Multi-account disambiguation)
phases.completion_note: Six changes. (1) NEW migration 20260513230000_scheduler_pending_candidates.sql — adds pending_candidates JSONB to customer_chat_sessions (nullable, no default). (2) database.types.ts manually patched with pending_candidates: Json | null in Row/Insert/Update — will be overwritten by user's regen post-migration-apply. (3) scheduler-step2-direct/index.ts: writes decision.data.candidates to row.pending_candidates when emitting show_multi_account_disambiguation (best-effort, structured console.warn on failure). (4) get-current-card.ts multi_account_disambiguation case now calls new parseCandidates() helper which defensively coerces JSONB and requires both customer_id (number) + recent_vehicle (non-empty string) per chat-design.md §3.5c PII protection. (5) NEW submit-multi-account-choice.ts — Zod discriminated union ('select' with selected_customer_id, or 'none_of_these'). select → sets customer_id + clears pending_candidates + calls callOtpResend (reuses scheduler-otp-direct 'resend' op as first-send) + advances to otp_pending. none_of_these → clears candidates + advances to no_match_choose_path. (6) WizardSurface.tsx: added MultiAccountDisambiguationCard import + multi_account_disambiguation switch case. npm run typecheck passes clean.

phases.title: Step 5 (Customer info edit) + Step 4 (New customer info) + Codex bug 3 fix
phases.completion_note: Wires Step 5 (returning customer info edit) AND Step 4 new-client (New customer info). Also fixes Codex's bug 3 (submitOtp data-shape issue) by routing Tekmetric customer profile fetch through the OTP verify endpoint. Seven changes: (1) scheduler-otp-direct/index.ts verify op: after verifyOtp success + customer_id set + edited_phones not yet populated, fetches customer profile via getCustomerById and stashes phones/emails/address + verified_first/last_name + primary_email on the row. Fail-soft (logs warn on failure; advances anyway). Skips fetch when edited_phones is non-null (Phase 12 Edit-from-Summary re-entry preservation). (2) scheduler-booking-direct/index.ts: added op='patch_customer' for Step 5 Tekmetric PATCH. Calls existing patchCustomer Deno helper (full phone array, primary email single-string, address streetAddress concat). Returns ok:false with tekmetric_error_text on 4xx/5xx. (3) booking-direct-client.ts: typed patchCustomer wrapper + PatchCustomerRequest/Response types. (4) get-current-card.ts: fixed parsePhones + parseEmails to read canonical V1/V2 shape ({phone_e164, is_primary} / {email, is_primary}) — they previously read Tekmetric-shape keys ({number, primary}) and would have returned [] for V1/V2 rows. Accepts legacy keys defensively for backwards-compat. (5) NEW submit-customer-info-edit.ts: submitCustomerInfoEditV2 — Zod-validates, diffs vs current row, skips Tekmetric PATCH on no-op or non-full verification, calls patch_customer for full+changes, always writes edited_* + advances to vehicle_pick. Fail-soft (Tekmetric failure → escalation). (6) NEW submit-new-customer-info.ts: submitNewCustomerInfoV2 — reads first_name/last_name/phone_e164 from row, calls create_customer op via booking-direct-client wrapper, handles phone_duplicate by bouncing to phone_name with bucket='returning', writes customer_id + edited_* + identity_verification_level='full' on success, advances to vehicle_pick. (7) WizardSurface.tsx: added customer_info_edit + new_customer_info switch cases wiring both heritage cards. npm run typecheck passes clean.
phases.summary: Migrate Step 5 (customer info edit for returning customers) + Step 4 (new customer info for new-client flow). Fixes Codex's bug 3 (submitOtp data-shape issue) by routing the Tekmetric profile fetch through scheduler-otp-direct's verify op.

phases.title: Step 6 (Vehicle pick + new vehicle drill-down)
phases.completion_note: Wires Step 6 (vehicle picker + add-new drill-down) on the new pattern. Six changes: (1) scheduler-booking-direct: added op='fetch_vehicles_for_customer' that calls lookupVehiclesForCustomer (Deno helper) for an existing customer_id, returns vehicles array {id, year, make, model, sub_model, license_plate, color}. Fail-soft with tekmetric_4xx/5xx error tag. (2) booking-direct-client: typed fetchVehiclesForCustomer wrapper + FetchVehiclesRequest/Response types. (3) get-current-card.ts vehicle_pick case: calls fetchVehiclesForCustomer when customer_id is set, builds vehicle labels via new buildVehicleLabel helper ('YYYY MAKE MODEL · PLATE' per chat-design.md §1196), filters out empty labels, fail-soft renders empty list on Tekmetric error. New customers (no customer_id yet) skip the fetch and render empty list + allow_add_new=true. allow_add_new=false for partial-verification customers per spec §3.5a. (4) NEW submit-vehicle-pick.ts: 'new' → advance to new_vehicle_form; numeric → write vehicle_id + advance to service_concern_picker. (5) NEW submit-new-vehicle.ts: Zod-validates year/make/model + optional plate/notes, reads customer_id from row, calls create_vehicle op (existing — Phase 6 already had this in booking-direct-client). On success: writes new_vehicle_info JSONB (year/make/model/plate/notes per chat-design.md §1281 'stored verbatim, no AI parsing') and advances to service_concern_picker. On Tekmetric 4xx/5xx: escalates with reason tag. (6) WizardSurface.tsx: added vehicle_pick + new_vehicle_form switch cases. Legacy show_new_customer_form returns in session-actions.ts:1067, 1137, 1301 are LEFT ALONE per phase scope — they're only used by /book and phase 16 removes them entirely with the rest of the AI-SDK surface. npm run typecheck passes clean.
phases.summary: Migrate Step 6 vehicle picker + new-vehicle add. Removes the three legacy 'show_new_customer_form' returns from session-actions.ts (Codex bug 1).

phases.title: Step 7.1 (Service + concern picker)
phases.summary: Migrate Step 7.1 — the chip-list picker that branches into either Step 8 (no-explanation path) or Step 7.2 (explanation path).

Wire heritage/ServiceAndConcernPicker(?) or use existing ServiceAndConcernPicker into WizardSurface.

Create submitServiceAndConcernPickerV2 — partitions picks into selected_simple_services + items requiring explanation; writes both to row; advances to 'concern_explanation' if any explanation-required item exists, else 'appointment_type' (skipping 7.2-7.6 entirely).
Note: phase 9 covers the actual diagnostic flow; this phase only handles the picker + branch decision.
verification: Manual: pick only routine chips → advance directly to appointment_type
Manual: pick a concern chip → advance to concern_explanation

phases.title: Steps 7.2-7.5 (Per-concern explanations + diagnostic + clarifications + testing approval)
phases.summary: Migrate the diagnostic Q&A flow — the FIRST real use of the LLM in the new architecture. Per-concern explanation cards → parallel diagnostic specialist calls → clarification questions → testing service approval.
tasks: Create scheduler-app/src/lib/scheduler/wizard/llm/diagnose-concern.ts — server-side helper that calls Claude Haiku 4.5 via @ai-sdk/anthropic generateObject with a strict Zod output schema { category, remaining_questions, recommended_testing_services }. Reads concern_questions catalog + testing_services from the DB. NOT exposed as a 'tool'; called directly.

Update getCurrentCard for step='concern_explanation' (renders per-concern card with chat-bubble lead-in from bubble-templates), step='diagnostic_loading' (waits while Promise.all of diagnoseConcern calls), step='clarification_question' (renders one question from the queue), step='testing_service_approval'.
Wire the existing heritage cards into WizardSurface for these steps.
Create submitExplanationV2 — appends to explanation_required_items; advances to next concern's card OR to diagnostic_loading if all collected.

Create runDiagnosticsV2 (called automatically by getCurrentCard when step='diagnostic_loading' arrives — or invoked from the action that just advanced there): Promise.all-fans out diagnoseConcern per item, aggregates clarification_questions_pending + recommended_testing_services, sets diagnostic_processing_complete=true, advances to clarification_question OR testing_service_approval.
Create submitClarificationAnswerV2 — drops the answered question from the pending queue; advances to next clarification OR to testing_service_approval when queue drains.
Create submitTestingApprovalV2 — writes approved/declined; advances to second_routine_pass.
verification: Manual: full diagnostic flow with a brake concern → see clarification questions → approve testing → advance
Verify: model output is parsed via Zod; on parse failure return graceful fallback (skip diagnostic recommendations)
Verify: parallel Promise.all completes for multi-concern picks

phases.title: Steps 10.1-10.2 (Summary + per-section edits + Tekmetric POST confirm)
phases.summary: Migrate the summary card + the edit-from-summary drill-downs + the Tekmetric POST confirmation. Fixes the summary-edit-routing-to-date_pick bug (Codex finding 6).
tasks: Update getCurrentCard for step='summary' — loads all row fields for the summary display + hold_expires_at countdown.
Wire heritage/SummaryCard into WizardSurface.

Create summary edit handlers: editInfo → back to customer_info_edit, editVehicle → back to vehicle_pick, editServices → back to service_concern_picker (and re-run diagnostic if concerns changed), editDateTime → back to date_pick. EACH increments summary_edit_attempts; at attempts >= 2 the next edit fires escalation.

Wait — re-check chat-design.md §10.1.5: 'After the 2nd edit cycle completes (returns to summary), a 3rd Edit tap is intercepted'. The current code uses >= 3 (allows 2 edits per the fix). Verify and align.

Create submitSummaryConfirmV2 — verifies hold still valid; if expired bounce to date_pick with hold_expired bubble. Else call scheduler-booking-direct op='confirm_booking' with built description; on success write appointment_id + appointment_confirmed_at; advance to customer_notes. On failure: retry once with 1s backoff; surface error + retry option.
